tasks.description: Hotfix on E-69: `sh install-ai-os.sh` fails at line 113 (process substitution) on macOS where /bin/sh is bash-in-POSIX-mode. BASH_VERSION is set so the existing guard passes, but `set -o posix` disables `< <(find ...)`. Fix: detect POSIX mode via SHELLOPTS and re-exec under proper bash, with fallback error if bash is absent. Add T-NODE-S05 regression coverage simulating the mode via `bash --posix`.
tasks.summary: E-69 hotfix (Tier 1). Root cause: on macOS /bin/sh is a copy of bash; running `sh install-ai-os.sh` sets BASH_VERSION (guard at line 6 passes) but enables `set -o posix` which disables process substitution, so `< <(find ...)` at line 113 in purge_orphans() syntax-errored. Fix in install-ai-os.sh replaces the simple BASH_VERSION guard with a two-stage detector: (a) BASH_VERSION absent → not bash, re-exec under bash; (b) BASH_VERSION set but `:$SHELLOPTS:` contains `:posix:` → bash-in-POSIX-mode, re-exec under bash. Fallback: structured error "installer requires bash (not sh/POSIX mode)" exit 1 when bash is absent on PATH. _aios_need_reexec helper var unset after use. Verified: `HOME=$SBOX sh install-ai-os.sh` now exits 0 with full install output (no syntax error). T-NODE-S05 added to tests/suites/installer_node_check_test.sh (5 assertions: re-exec block references SHELLOPTS, uses exec bash, fallback names bash; behavioural sub-test runs `bash --posix install-ai-os.sh` against sandbox HOME and asserts exit 0 + no syntax-error stderr). Full suite: 1292 → 1299 PASS.
